description: Formal invariant specifications for critical cryptographic operations — prove-or-block
policy: Every critical operation must have: ≥1 algebraic identity verified, ≥1 boundary test, CT flag if applicable, linked test IDs. No untested critical operation permitted.

preconditions: nonce k is derived deterministically via RFC 6979
postconditions: signature (r, s) where 0 < r < n and 0 < s < n
s is in low-S form: s <= n/2
algebraic_identities: sign(msg, sk) produces identical output for identical inputs (determinism)

preconditions: signature (r, s) where 0 < r < n and 0 < s < n
public_key is a valid point on secp256k1
postconditions: returns true iff signature is valid for message under pubkey
rejects high-S signatures when strict mode enabled
rejects signature with r=0 or s=0
algebraic_identities: verify(msg, sign(msg, sk), pubkey(sk)) == true for all valid sk
verify(msg, (r, n-s), pk) may differ from verify(msg, (r, s), pk) under strict low-S
verify(msg_a, sig_b, pk) == false when msg_a != msg_b (with overwhelming probability)

preconditions: aux_rand is exactly 32 bytes (BIP-340 tagged hash nonce)
algebraic_identities: verify(msg, sign(msg, sk, aux), xonly_pubkey(sk)) == true
sign(msg, sk, aux) deterministic for same inputs
boundary_conditions: sk with odd Y coordinate → key negation must occur

preconditions: pubkey is a valid x-only 32-byte encoding
postconditions: returns true iff BIP-340 verification equation holds
algebraic_identities: verify(msg, sign(msg, sk, aux), xonly(sk)) == true for valid sk
verify(msg_a, sig_b, pk) == false when msg_a != msg_b
boundary_conditions: invalid x-coordinate (no valid Y) → must reject
all BIP-340 test vectors from BIP document

preconditions: public_key is valid point on secp256k1, not infinity
algebraic_identities: ecdh(sk, pk) deterministic for same inputs

preconditions: child index: 0 ≤ i < 2^31 for normal, 2^31 ≤ i < 2^32 for hardened
postconditions: derived key is valid secp256k1 secret key (0 < k < n)
algebraic_identities: derive(seed, path) deterministic for same inputs
boundary_conditions: hardened child resulting in invalid key (must retry with next index)

preconditions: input scalar s where 0 ≤ s < n
postconditions: if s ≠ 0: return s^(-1) mod n such that s * s^(-1) ≡ 1 (mod n)
boundary_conditions: s = n → equivalent to 0 after reduction

coverage_summary.note: All operations have dynamic testing with algebraic identity verification. SMT/Lean formal proofs are a future enhancement target.
